title: Trace slow and error spans in Tempo

blocks.content:
Traces connect user-visible latency to which span consumed the time. You already fixed the incident window in UTC; now you locate representative error and slow paths.

To query traces, complete the following steps.

1. In the main Grafana navigation on the left, open **Explore**.

   If the navigation is collapsed, open it with the menu control, then choose **Explore**.

1. Select your Tempo data source.

   For example `grafanacloud-demoinfra-traces`.

1. Set the time range to the same absolute window you used for metrics and logs.

1. Run the following TraceQL query.

```traceql
{ status = error }
```

   Note how many traces match and which span names recur.

1. Run the following query to narrow to slow error spans.

```traceql
{ span:duration > 200ms && status = error }
```

1. Open one matching trace into the waterfall view.

1. Fill the trace table from the waterfall.

| Capture | Your value |
| --- | --- |
| Root span + total duration | |
| Dominant child span | |
| Child span as % of trace time | |

1. Run the following query.

```traceql
{ resource.service.name = "quickpizza" && span:duration > 200ms }
```

   If the resource attribute differs in your stack, substitute the value your services use.

| Reflection | Your answer (1–2 sentences) |
| --- | --- |
| Root span vs k6 timing diverged—plausible causes? | |

Check your work: you can move on when the time range matches your k6 window, the trace table is filled, and you answered the reflection row. If TraceQL returns nothing, confirm the Tempo data source, service labels, and window, then open the troubleshooting topics for this milestone.

In the next milestone you combine your notes into a postmortem-style narrative and complete the reflection list.
